preamble: A shift from the bilabial [b] toward a velar or palatal stop typically occurs through palatalization before front vowels: a following [j] or [i] raises the tongue body, shifting the primary place of articulation away from the lips. In Moldavian Romanian, [b] before a front vowel yields the palatal stop [ɟ] (spelled 'gh-'). In Sakata (Bantu), labial stops developed a secondary velar component through areal diffusion, yielding doubly-articulated labial-velar stops [gb].

examples.note: Labial [b] palatalized before front vowel [i], shifting primary articulation to the palate.
Simple labial stop developed a secondary velar component via areal diffusion, yielding a doubly-articulated labial-velar.
